_meta.purpose: Machine-readable consolidation of data_prep/style_profiles/*.md, for the synth_persons.py style layer. Each profile encodes the RENDERABLE conventions of one publisher/era so generated NYC lines are volume-accurate instead of one generic voice. Distilled from visual sampling (session 1-4, 2026-06-19/20).
_meta.consumed_by: data_prep/synth_persons.py (render_nyc / make_nyc style layer -- not yet wired as of this file's creation)

field_notes.year_range: [first, last] printed years the profile covers; generator selects a profile whose range + city/borough fit the target.
field_notes.column_count: print columns (structural metadata; not rendered, but tags the era's density).
field_notes.widow_forms: weighted templates. {hf}=husband given name; {addr}=an address; '' slot = the no-name variant. THE biggest cross-publisher differentiator.
field_notes.markers: home = the home-address marker; rear; works_in_manhattan = Brooklyn commuter notation; ditto = repeated-token convention ('' = full repeat, no ditto).
field_notes.abbreviations: canonical -> printed form, from the volume's abbreviations key (or inferred). legend_completeness says how trustworthy.
field_notes.entry_template: human-readable field order (for reference / few-shot), NOT a format string.
field_notes.samples: verbatim lines from the actual volume (validation + optional few-shot anchors).

samples: Earnest James, sign painter 206 Pearl h. 19 Cherry
Easton Charles cot. broker 63 Wall c. Pearl h. 62 Rivington
East Ann widow of Josiah, 171 Division
Rivers Augusta, w. of Jas. R. boardng. 368 Broome
Rivers widow Rachel, 45 Carmine
longworth_manhattan_1830s.notes: Comma after given name(s) but NOT between occupation and workplace address (distinct from Doggett/Lain). 1797 put the comma after the SURNAME with do.=ditto street; by 1820 it moved to after the given name. Sparse abbreviations, mostly spelled out (old style).

samples: Dewint John, merchant, 12, Duke-street
Draper Geo. doctor, &c. 47, Han.-square
Douglass Geo. & S. merchants, 233, Q. st.
Franks D. conveyancer, &c. 66, Broadw.
franks_newyork_1786.notes: First NYC directory ever (82pp). ONE address per entry (no home/work split, no 'h' marker). Street NUMBER precedes street NAME. No abbreviations key, no ditto; loose conventions (some entries drop commas, some lack occupation).

samples: Fowler N. Hill, drygoods, 167 Pearl, h 46 Fourth
FOX BALDWIN N. grocer, 32 Water, h 147 ch'ry
Fox Ann, widow of Jeremy, 17 pl
Fox Charles, (co'd) seamn, 139 W. Fifteenth
SCHRODER HENRY, engraver
doggett_manhattan_1840s.notes: Prominent/notable persons (and firms) in ALL CAPS. Widow spelled out 'widow of <husband first>'. Race designation '(co'd)' = colored, in parens after the name. Denser '61,333 names' product; no explicit abbreviations key found in front matter.

samples: Evans Ira P, grocer 79 Front NY h Union c Court
EVANS MARTIN, druggist (Alderman 7th ward) Myrtle c ...
Fitzpatrick Mary, widow of Michael, 197 York
Slade John, widow 58 Fulton
*Flood Alvy, barber 82 Atlantic h 177 Adams
hearne_brooklyn_1850s.notes: Brooklyn street-relation grammar: many entries give location relative to cross/named streets (n/c/b/op) instead of a house number. 'NY' = works in Manhattan (commuter), usually + 'h' Brooklyn home. Officials in ALL CAPS with office in parens. '*' prefix on some names = uncertain (late-add/removed?).

samples: Millan John, candy store Fulton c Fleet
Mullen Mary, widow 5 Halls alley r Furman st
Murray Joseph, shipping merchant N Y h 79 Pierrepont
Nally John, foundry man Myrtle n Clinton
spooner_brooklyn_1840s.notes: Same Brooklyn street-relation grammar as Hearne (adjacent era) -- the early-Brooklyn predecessor. Pre-Lain. Cross-references [hearne_brooklyn_1850s].

trow_manhattan_1890s.entry_template: Surname Given[, occupation][, employer/business][, h HomeAddr]
samples: Dupp Wm. painter, h 305 E. 29th
Duppler Chas. tngr. 33, 3d av. h 240 E. 10th
— Chas. cooper, h 5205, 3d av.
Dupre Felton, books, 549, 5th av.
trow_manhattan_1890s.notes: DITTO DASH: a leading '—' repeats the prior surname on continuation lines (unlike Lain, which repeats the full surname). 3 columns (preface-stated). col 2->3 transition was 1857->1859 (1857 vols are 2-col). Listing pages carry ad strips top & bottom. Legend is ~6 mini-columns -- transcribe fully for production.

lain_brooklyn_1880s.entry_template: Surname Given[s][, wid. SpouseGiven][, Occupation][, Employer/WorkAddr][, h HomeAddr]
samples: Aab George, machinist, h 294 Stockton
Abbey Westminster S. grocer, 61 Front N.Y. h 229 Union
Adams Mary, wid. Philip, h 343 Pacific
Adams Ellen, wid. Thomas, grocer, 71 N. 7th
ADAMS HENRY H. county treas'r, 13 Court
lain_brooklyn_1880s.notes: FULL legend transcribed from the abbreviations key (canvas 1, = listing-start page). Widow = 'wid. <husband given>' (the original synth→real failure case). Full surname repeated every line (NO ditto dash, unlike Trow). Prominent persons ALL CAPS. 'N.Y.' after a work address = commutes to Manhattan. Imprint: J. Lain 1858 -> Geo. T. Lain 1869 -> Lain & Co. -> Lain & Healy 1890s. Held-out eval volume: 1897BPL.

samples: Hallett William D, undertaker, h 25 Main
Halliday Augusta, widow George, h 53 Main
Hamilton James C (clerk, N.Y.), h Sandford ave nr Union
Harvey Andrew, carpenter, 87 West Amity, h do
Harris Deborah, widow, h 129 Washington
boyd_flushing_1880s.notes: Small-town: addresses frequently OMIT house numbers (street name only). Widow = 'widow <husband given>' (no 'of'). 'h do' = ditto (home repeats the line above). '(N.Y.)' in parens after occupation = works in NYC. Volume's 2nd half is a classified BUSINESS directory (not a persons target).

upington_brooklyn_1900s.entry_template: Surname Given, occupation[, employer/work addr], h HomeAddr
upington_brooklyn_1900s.notes: Post-1900 Brooklyn, dense 2-col with marginal vertical ad strips (same layout family as Lain). Abbreviations key NOT reached in the front-20 sample (ads lead) -- legend is inferred from the Lain lineage and needs a deeper front scan to transcribe fully. 'Volume LXXXIII' in 1906 (long-running series, distinct from Lain). Heavy duplicate scans across the master list.
